DP.202.0003:
Welcome to the Contest Hall.\f
I can help you with registering
for a Super Contest.\f
DP.202.0004:
Would you like to enter a Pokémon
in a Contest?
DP.202.0005: What would you like to know about?
DP.202.0007:
Welcome to the Contest Hall!\f
This is the reception counter for
linked Super Contests.\f
You may join a group of friends to
enter your Pokémon in the same\r
Super Contest over a link.\f
DP.202.0011:
We look forward to your participation
another time!
DP.202.0012:
Would you like to
enter a link Contest?
DP.202.0013:
Two to four players may compete
over a link.\f
First, choose the Contest you wish
to enter.\f
Next, make sure the other people in
your group choose the same Contest.\f
Then, the players each choose a
Pokémon to enter the Contest, and\r
that’s it for registration!\f
After that, the Contest starts in
the usual way.\f
We hope you’ll try it out.\f
DP.202.0014:
A Pokémon Super Contest involves
four Trainers.\f
Each one enters a Pokémon to
compete against the others.\f
Judging is conducted in three
categories: Visual, Dance, and\r
Acting.\f
The Pokémon scoring the highest in
the three categories is the winner.\f
If this is your first time, it is
recommended you try some practice\r
sessions before entering a Contest.\f
The registration counter for practice
sessions is at the far right.\f
DP.202.0015:
In the Visual Competition, Pokémon
are rated on their appearance.\f
Scoring is based on votes cast by
the audience.\f
For details, please inquire at the
practice counter on the right.\f
DP.202.0016:
In the Dance Competition, the Pokémon
will get to show off their footwork.\f
Try to put on coordinated dance
performances with everyone!\f
For details, please inquire at the
practice counter on the right.\f
DP.202.0017:
In the Acting Competition, the
Pokémon will perform their moves in\r
front of Judges.\f
Try to get your Pokémon to put on
performances that stand out from the\r
rest of the pack!\f
For details, please inquire at the
practice counter on the right.\f
DP.202.0018:
There are five Contests in all:
Cool, Beauty, Cute, Smart, and Tough.\f
Be sure to choose the Contest that
best suits the Pokémon you want to\r
enter with.\f
DP.202.0019:
There are four different ranks to
the Pokémon Super Contests.\f
The four ranks are Normal, Great,
Ultra, and Master.\f
The Normal Rank is open for entry
by any Pokémon.\f
A Pokémon winning a Normal Rank
Contest may move up to the Great\r
Rank in the same category.\f
Likewise, win a Great Contest and
become eligible for the Ultra Rank.\f
An Ultra Rank winner may move up to
the Master Rank in the same way.\f
A Master Rank winner may enter
Master Rank Contests as often as\r
the Trainer wishes.\f
DP.202.0020:
Which Pokémon would you like to
enter?\f
DP.202.0022:
OK! Your Pokémon has been accepted
for entry!\f
Your entry number is [0132,0000]!\f
DP.202.0023:
We look forward to your participation
another time!
DP.202.0027:
Which rank would you like to
challenge?
DP.202.0028:
Which Contest would you like to
challenge?
DP.202.0029: Would you like to cancel your entry?
DP.202.0064:
The Contest will start right away.
This way, please!\f
DP.202.0068:
Right this way, please!
Good luck!\f
DP.202.0069:
MC: We are about to get under
way with this [0123,0000] Pokémon\r
[0122,0001]!\f
I’m Dexter, and I’ll be serving as
the MC and as one of the Judges!\f
The results of judging will be
announced at the end, so please\r
bear with me!\f
DP.202.0070:
Let me introduce our contestants!\f
Entry number 1!
Here’s [0103,0000]!\f
DP.202.0073:
And last but not least, entry
number 4! [0103,0000]!\f
DP.202.0074:
Let’s begin by having everyone get in
proper attire for evaluations.\f
Contestants, dress up your Pokémon
for the Visual Competition!\f
DP.202.0077:
Let’s recognize our winner!
[0103,0000]’s [0101,0001]!\f
Our winner is awarded the
[0127,0002]!\f
DP.202.0078:
We look forward to your next
Contest challenge!\f
DP.202.0079:
We expect great things from the
winning team of [0103,0000] and\r
[0101,0001]!\f
DP.202.0082:
Thank you for participating.
We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0111:
Hi! This is the registration counter
for practice sessions.\f
You may practice in the categories
of Visual, Dance, and Acting.\f
DP.202.0112:
Would you like to join a practice
session?
DP.202.0116: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0117: What would you like to know about?
DP.202.0123: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0124:
A Contest involves judging in three
categories: Visual, Dance, and\r
Acting.\f
Here you may practice each of these
categories before you actually enter\r
a real Contest.\f
There’s just one thing. Your Pokémon
must know at least two moves.\f
Otherwise, it will not qualify for our
Super Contests.\f
DP.202.0130:
Judging here is based on the visual
appeal of Pokémon.\f
Scoring is based on voting by the
audience.\f
The more popular the Pokémon, the
higher its score!\f
The Pokémon are scored in two
categories: Condition Points and\r
Dress-up Points.\f
DP.202.0131:
Condition Points are scored on
the Pokémon’s condition in one of\r
five categories.\f
The five conditions are: Cool, Beauty,
Cute, Smart, and Tough.\f
These conditions can be enhanced by
feeding Poffins to your Pokémon.\f
If the audience likes what they see,
[FF00,0001]♡[FF00,0000] marks will appear.\f
Obviously, the more [FF00,0001]♡[FF00,0000] marks there
are, the more popular the Pokémon.\f
DP.202.0132:
Dress-up Points are awarded for
fashionably dressing up your Pokémon\r
with Accessories.\f
Before you begin, you will be given
a fashion theme.\f
Your dressed-up Pokémon will be
scored on how well it matches the\r
assigned theme.\f
The audience’s approval will appear
as pink [FF00,0001]♡[FF00,0000] marks.\f
Be sure to position the Accessories
properly and attach them firmly.\r
They won’t stay on otherwise.\f
There is a time limit of 60 seconds.\f
Get your Pokémon dressed up within
that time.\f
DP.202.0134: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0140:
In a Dance Competition, the four
contestants take on different parts.\f
One becomes the main dancer, and the
remaining three are backup dancers.\f
The main dancer scores points by
performing inspired dance steps\r
that the others can’t copy.\f
The backup dancers earn high scores
by duplicating the dance steps\r
laid down by the main dancer.\f
DP.202.0141:
Your Pokémon will first appear as
a backup dancer.\f
Make it dance using the same timing
as the main dancer.\f
Use the castanets to show your
Pokémon which direction the main\r
dancer turned.\f
DP.202.0142:
The main dancer rotates with a
backup dancer so everyone gets\r
a turn.\f
When your Pokémon is the main dancer,
show it how to dance!\f
DP.202.0143:
The melody will be shown playing on
the red and blue panels.\f
The panel colors show who should be
dancing.\f
The main dancer can dance while the
melody is in the blue panels.\f
Backup dancers must dance only
during the red panels.\f
The dancers must perform the
required number of dance steps\r
during their time slots.\f
DP.202.0144: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0150:
In the Acting Competition, four
Pokémon will perform their moves for\r
our panel of Judges.\f
The more a Pokémon stands out, the
higher it scores.\f
Each Pokémon will get the chance to
perform four times.\f
DP.202.0151:
Every move has one or more [FF00,0001]♡[FF00,0000] marks
that indicate the acting score.\f
The more [FF00,0001]♡[FF00,0000] marks obtained, the
higher the Pokémon scores.\f
However, Pokémon are not permitted
to perform the same move two turns\r
in a row.\f
DP.202.0152:
There are three Judges in all.\f
You get to choose which one you
want to judge your Pokémon.\f
Try not to have your Pokémon scored
by the same Judge as other Pokémon\r
so it can stand out more.\f
If your Pokémon leaves a strong
impression, it will be rated highly,\r
earning it more [FF00,0001]♡[FF00,0000] marks.\f
DP.202.0153:
Voltage shows the excitement level
of the Judges.\f
In a Cool Contest, if a Pokémon uses
a Cool move, Voltage goes up.\f
However, if a Cute or Smart move is
performed, the Voltage goes down.\f
If the Voltage reaches 5 points, the
Pokémon earns bonus points.\f
If the Special Judge in the center
was chosen, 8 bonus points are\r
awarded when the Voltage peaks.\f
For the other Judges, 5 bonus points
are awarded.\f
DP.202.0154: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0159: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0160:
Which Contest would you like to
challenge?
DP.202.0167: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0175:
Which Pokémon would you like to
enter?\f
DP.202.0177:
OK, your Pokémon has been registered
for a practice session.\f
Its entry number is [0132,0000].
The practice will start right away.\f
DP.202.0178:
Right this way, please.
Good luck!\f
DP.202.0179:
Your Pokémon will be evaluated by the
audience.\f
You can enhance its condition by
feeding it Poffins.\f
You will also need to dress up your
Pokémon using Accessories.\f
Would you like to enter a practice
session?
DP.202.0182: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0183:
OK, let me show you in.
Right this way, please.\f
DP.202.0184:
Thanks for participating!
Your Pokémon was quite popular!\r
It’s ready for a real Contest!\f
Or, would you like to practice again?
DP.202.0185:
Thank you for participating!
It’s too bad how things turned out...\f
May I suggest feeding Poffins to your
Pokémon to improve its condition?\f
You should also use Accessories
matched to the dress-up themes to\r
boost your Pokémon’s appeal.\f
Would you like to practice again?
DP.202.0188: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0194:
Thanks for participating!
Your Pokémon danced quite well!\r
It’s ready for a real Contest!\f
Or, would you like to practice dancing
again?
DP.202.0195:
Thank you for participating!
It’s too bad how things turned out...\f
The key is to remember which ways
the main dancer turned, then make\r
the same moves at the same time.\f
Would you like to practice dancing
again?
DP.202.0198: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0204:
Thanks for participating!\f
Your Pokémon attracted a good deal
of attention!\f
Remember, there are five kinds of
Contests: Cool, Beauty, Cute,\r
Smart, and Tough.\f
You can enter your Pokémon in
Contests that best suit them.\f
Try getting your Pokémon to stand
out in these Contests!\f
Would you like to practice again?
DP.202.0205:
Thank you for participating!
It’s too bad how things turned out...\f
A key point is choosing which Judge
you want to check your Pokémon’s\r
performance.\f
If your Pokémon performs for a Judge
who hasn’t been chosen by another\r
Pokémon, it will score higher.\f
Pick a Judge who will only look
at your Pokémon... Aim for that!\f
Would you like to practice again?
DP.202.0209: What would you like to know about?
DP.202.0211:
Right this way, please!
Good luck!\f
DP.202.0212:
DS Wireless Communications will
be launched.
DP.202.0215: Would you like to cancel your entry?
DP.202.0218: We look forward to your next visit!
DP.202.0219:
One person in the group needs to
choose “BECOME LEADER.”\f
Everyone else must choose
“JOIN GROUP.”
